questions.question: Chennai Metro inks ₹1,205-crore contract agreement with which company for stations?
questions.explanation: Chennai Metro Rail Ltd signed a contract agreement with Tata Projects Ltd for a value ₹1,205 crore. The contract is part of JICA funding for Phase-II. The scope of work involves construction of four underground stations and works other than the diaphragm wall of two underground stations.

questions.question: ‘National IPR Policy’ is associated with which Union Ministry?
questions.explanation: The Intellectual Property Rights Policy Management (IPRPM) framework covers various types of intellectual property rights, including: (i) Patents, (ii) Trade mark, (iii) Industrial Designs, (iv) Copyrights, (v) Geographical Indications, (vi) Semiconductor Integrated Circuit Layout Design, (vii) Trade Secret, and (viii) Plant Varieties. This comprehensive framework, known as the National IPR Policy 2016, consolidates all these intellectual property rights under one unified vision document.

questions.question: Which Union Ministry issued the ‘IRC codes and guidelines’?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways

answer.label: A
answer.text: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways

questions.explanation: The IRC (Indian Road Congress) codes and guidelines were issued recently by the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways. These guidelines, approved by Union Minister for Road Transport and Highways Nitin Gadkari, aim to improve road safety by incorporating best practices and global standards, providing better visibility and intuitive guidance to drivers.

questions.question: Cymbidium lancifoliu, a rare species of orchid has been found in which state other than the north-east states?
questions.explanation: A highly uncommon terrestrial orchid species known as Cymbidium lancifolium was found in the Chopta valley of the Chamoli district, situated in the Western Himalayan region of Uttarakhand. Before this discovery, the presence of this species had been documented solely in the northeastern regions of India and Bhutan.

questions.question: Mt Brammah is a mountain massif located in which Indian state/UT?
questions.explanation: Brammah is a mountain massif located in the Kishtwar Himalayas of Jammu and Kashmir, India, situated east of Kishtwar town and in close proximity to the Himachal Pradesh border. An Indian team has made history by successfully reaching the summit of Mt Brammah 1, which is standing at a height of 6,416 metres.

questions.question: Which organisation has asked schools to consider Indian languages as teaching medium?
questions.explanation: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) asked its affiliated schools to consider providing the option of Indian languages mentioned in Schedule 8 of the Constitution as a medium of instruction, in addition to the existing options, from pre-primary classes to Class 12. The move is a part of the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, which envisages the use of mother tongue or regional or Indian languages as the medium of instruction until at least Grade 5, and preferably till Grade 8 and beyond.

questions.question: Where is the International Seabed Authority (ISA) headquartered?
questions.explanation: Jamaica-based International Seabed Authority’s member nations agreed on a two-year roadmap for the adoption of deep sea mining regulations. The ISA, an intergovernmental body tasked with protecting the seabed, and its member states have spent the last decade trying to hash out a mining code for the possible exploitation of nickel, cobalt and copper in deep seabed areas that fall outside of national jurisdictions.

questions.question: Which country hosted the ‘Talisman Sabre exercises’?
questions.explanation: Recently, live-fire drills featuring the American-made HIMARS missile system were conducted at the Shoalwater Bay military complex in Queensland, Australia, showcasing its recent acquisition by the Australian Defence Force. The drills are part of the biennial Talisman Sabre exercises, involving over 30,000 troops from various countries, including Japan, France, Germany, and South Korea.

questions.question: Te’omim Cave, which was seen in the news, is located in which city?
questions.explanation: The Te’omim Cave in Jerusalem was found to host more than 100 ceramic lamps squeezed into the cave’s crevices. In a new study, researchers propose that these objects were probably employed for summoning deceased spirits and uncovering their hidden knowledge, a ritual referred to as necromancy.

questions.question: ‘Khelo India – National Programme for Development of Sports’ Scheme is being implemented from which year?
questions.explanation: ‘Khelo India – National Programme for Development of Sports’ Scheme is a central sector scheme implemented since 2016. The ‘Promotion of rural and indigenous/tribal games’ sub-component of this scheme, assistance is provided to Mallakhamb, Kalaripayyattu, Gatka, Thang-Ta, Silambam, and Yogasan in various aspects. These include infrastructure development, equipment support, coach appointments, coach training, and athlete scholarships.
